document.source_title: IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians’ de Kock, Raj Bawa ruled out of remainder of season
document.summary: Quinton de Kock and Raj Angad Bawa of the Mumbai Indians have been ruled out for the remainder of the IPL 2026 season due to injuries. De Kock is sidelined with a tendon injury in his left wrist, while Bawa has a ligament tear in his right thumb. Both players will continue their rehabilitation at home with support from the Mumbai Indians medical team. The team has already been eliminated from playoff contention and has two matches left this season.
key_takeaways: Quinton de Kock ruled out due to a tendon injury in his left wrist.
Raj Angad Bawa ruled out due to a ligament tear in his right thumb.
Mumbai Indians have been eliminated from playoff contention.
De Kock played three matches this season, starting with an unbeaten 112.
Bawa also made three appearances for the team this season.
Mumbai Indians will announce replacements as per IPL guidelines.
document.why_it_matters: The injuries to key players like de Kock and Bawa highlight the physical demands of the IPL season and the impact on team performance, especially for a franchise like Mumbai Indians that has high expectations.
